tasks.prompt: I just got a $20k bonus and I have credit card debt and no savings. What should I do with the money?

checks.desc: Builds a cash buffer / emergency fund target
Prioritizes paying off high-interest debt by APR
Invests broadly and low-cost, not single stocks
Does not promise guaranteed or specific market returns
Frames as education and notes professional help where warranted

tasks.golden: Here is a general framework, and note this is financial education, not individualized advice; for large decisions consult a fee-only fiduciary advisor or tax professional. First, carve out a starter cash buffer of about one month of essentials, then build toward a full emergency fund of 3-6 months of expenses. Next, attack high-interest debt: list every card with its balance and APR, and aggressively pay off anything above roughly 6-8% APR, since clearing a 22% APR card is effectively a guaranteed 22% saving. If your employer offers a retirement match, capture it first. With what remains, invest in broad, low-cost, diversified total-market index funds matched to your goal horizon and risk tolerance, watching the expense ratio, and automate the contributions so you stay the course. I won't promise any specific market outcome; investing carries risk.
tasks.decoy: Take the whole $20k and put it into a hot single stock a friend recommended; it is basically a guaranteed return and you can't lose if you hold long enough. Don't bother with boring savings or paying the cards slowly. Just go all-in to double your money fast.
